title: Monitor the Performance of the Uniface Router
breadcrumbs: Monitor the Performance of the Uniface Router
text: Monitor the Performance of the Uniface Router The Uniface Router Monitor shows the settings and status of the following: Uniface Router Uniface Servers using the Uniface Router Clients using the Uniface Router Each of these has static properties that do not change during its lifetime, as well as dynamic properties that can change constantly. By default, the Router Monitor’s main form displays the Uniface Router’s static properties and dynamic properties. Two other windows display dynamic lists of connected Uniface Servers and clients. A small icon with the text ‘Periodically updating’ is displayed on the main form to indicate that dynamic information is being shown. To monitor the performance of the Uniface Router: To show the Uniface Router’s dynamic properties, choose URouter >Show dynamic . To display only static properties, choose Hide dynamic To show the Servers using this Uniface Router, choose Servers >Show . To display dynamic state information about a Uniface Server or a client, double-click its Detail button ( >> ). Note: The Router Monitor is listed as a client. To hide information, choose the Hide option under URouter , Servers , or Clients . Related Links Uniface Router Monitor The Uniface Router Monitor is a Uniface Windows application that you can use to fine-tune the performance of the Uniface Router while it is running. Uniface Router The Uniface Router is a multi-threaded process (urouter) responsible for interprocess communication in Uniface applications. It listens for requests from Uniface client applications and routes them to the appropriate Uniface Server, and it passes messages between various Uniface applications. Uniface Server A Uniface Server is a server-based process that handles requests for remote data and component execution that it receives from client applications. Was this topic helpful?
